# billing

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> TypeSpec schema-only folder defining the v1 billing API surface: billing profiles, customer overrides, and invoice lifecycle endpoints. All files compile into api/openapi.yaml via `make gen-api`; no runtime code lives here.

## Patterns

**@friendlyName on every model, union, enum, and interface** — Every public declaration carries @friendlyName to produce stable, human-readable Go type names. Missing @friendlyName causes autogenerated names that break SDK contracts on schema change. (`@friendlyName("BillingProfileCustomerOverride")
model BillingProfileCustomerOverride { ... }`)
**@visibility lifecycle decorators on every field** — Fields must be annotated with Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update as appropriate. Fields without @visibility default to all lifecycles and expose internal state in create/update payloads. (`@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)
supplier: BillingParty;`)
**Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic types** — All union variants use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) so the discriminator field is inlined rather than nested in an envelope object. (`@discriminated(#{ discriminatorPropertyName: "type", envelope: "none" })
union BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignment {
  subscription: BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignmentSubscription,
  anchored: BillingWorkflowCollectionAlignmentAnchored,
}`)
**Spread (...) for model composition instead of inheritance (extends)** — Models reuse shared fields via spread (e.g. ...global.Resource, ...ResourceTimestamps, ...OmitProperties<T, ...>). Using extends where spread is expected changes property semantics and breaks generated SDK types. (`model BillingProfileCustomerOverride {
  ...OmitProperties<ResourceTimestamps, "deletedAt">;
  billingProfileId?: ULID;
}`)
**Explicit @operationId on every interface operation** — Every operation must carry @operationId to produce stable Go function names in the generated server stub (api/api.gen.go). Omitting it causes autogenerated names that may collide or change across TypeSpec compiler versions. (`@get
@operationId("getBillingProfile")
get(@path id: ULID): BillingProfile | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;`)
**Namespace OpenMeter on all declarations** — Every model, union, enum, and interface must be declared inside namespace OpenMeter. Missing namespace causes the symbol to land in the global scope and fail to compile as part of the package. (`namespace OpenMeter;

@friendlyName("BillingProfile")
model BillingProfile { ... }`)
**main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files** — The package's main.tsp imports the invoices/main.tsp child and every sibling .tsp file. A new file added without an import in main.tsp compiles in isolation but is invisible to the full package output. (`// main.tsp
import "./invoices/main.tsp";
import "./profile.tsp";
import "./customeroverride.tsp";
import "./invoices.tsp";`)

## Key Files

| File                   | Role                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Watch For                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `main.tsp`             | Package entry point: imports all sibling and child .tsp files and declares the OpenMeter namespace usage directives.                                                                                                                                                    | Adding a new .tsp file without a matching import here — the file silently produces no output.                                                                                                                                                    |
| `profile.tsp`          | Defines BillingProfile, BillingProfileCreate, BillingProfileReplaceUpdate, BillingWorkflow and its sub-settings (collection, invoicing, payment, tax), the BillingProfileApps / BillingProfileAppReferences union, and the CRUD interface for /api/v1/billing/profiles. | BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses `is TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile>` — adding a field to BillingProfile without considering its visibility on the replace model will leak read-only fields into the update payload. |
| `customeroverride.tsp` | Defines customer override CRUD at /api/v1/billing/customers/{customerId} and the BillingProfileCustomerOverrideWithDetails response that merges override + base profile.                                                                                                | BillingProfileCustomerWorkflowOverride has app fields annotated @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — they must never appear in create/update inputs.                                                                                               |
| `invoices.tsp`         | Defines invoice listing/get/delete/update/action endpoints across three interface groups (InvoicesEndpoints, InvoiceEndpoints, CustomerInvoiceEndpoints) and request/response models for all invoice actions (void, approve, advance, retry, snapshot-quantities).      | VoidInvoiceLineAction is a discriminated union with envelope:none — adding a new void action type requires both a new arm in the union AND a new model with a matching `type` literal field.                                                     |
| `invoices/main.tsp`    | Child package entry: imports all invoice sub-models (invoice.tsp, discounts.tsp, party.tsp, pay.tsp, tax.tsp, docref.tsp). Changes to invoice entity shape go in the child package.                                                                                     | Do not define invoice entity fields in the parent invoices.tsp — that file only defines endpoints and action input/output models.                                                                                                                |

## Anti-Patterns

- Adding runtime or business logic — this folder is schema-only; all logic belongs in openmeter/billing/
- Editing generated files (api/openapi.yaml, api/api.gen.go) instead of the .tsp source
- Omitting @friendlyName on new models, unions, or enums — causes unstable generated Go type names that break SDK contracts
- Adding fields without @visibility decorators — fields default to all lifecycles and expose internal state on create/update
- Using extends for model composition instead of spread (...) or OmitProperties — changes property inheritance semantics in generated output

## Decisions

- **GOBL schema as the structural baseline for invoice models with deliberate omissions documented in comments** — OpenMeter invoices are serialized to GOBL format; keeping the TypeSpec schema structurally aligned with GOBL reduces conversion code in openmeter/billing/ and makes schema evolution traceable.
- **Visibility-controlled fields instead of separate request/response types** — Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Read/Create/Update) on a single model reduces duplication and ensures the OpenAPI generator derives correct request/response schemas automatically without maintaining parallel type hierarchies.
- **Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic workflow alignment and void-action types** — envelope:none inlines the discriminator field directly in the object, matching the Go domain types in openmeter/billing/ which use a `Type` string field rather than a wrapper object.

## Example: Add a new billing workflow action endpoint with a polymorphic input

```
// In invoices.tsp, inside InvoiceEndpoints:
@post
@route("/my-action")
@summary("Perform my action")
@operationId("myInvoiceAction")
myAction(
  @path invoiceId: ULID,
  @body request: MyActionInput,
): Invoice | NotFoundError | CommonErrors;

// New models in the same file:
@friendlyName("MyActionInput")
model MyActionInput {
  @visibility(Lifecycle.Create)
  action: MyActionType;
// ...
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
